title: Modellus Model
description: JSON schema for a Modellus model file produced by shell.serialize().

agentGuidance.description: Guidance for AI agents reading or building Modellus models. This section is schema-only documentation; it never appears in real model files.

readingAModel.description: How to interpret an existing model file step by step.

step1_identity.const: Read properties.name and properties.description to understand the stated intent.

step2_independent.const: Check properties.independent: 'name' is the simulation variable (usually 't' for time); 'start', 'end', 'step' define the numerical range.

step3_expressions.const: Collect all ExpressionShape items from the board array. Each shape's properties.expression is a \displaylines{} block — split on \\\\ to get individual statements. Parse each statement using expression-schema.

step4_termTypes.const: Classify terms: DIFFERENTIAL (dx/dt = ...) are dynamical state variables. PARAMETER (constant RHS, no dependencies) are physical constants. FUNCTION are derived quantities recomputed each step.

step5_initialValues.const: properties.initialValuesByCase keys are 1-based case numbers as strings. Each value is a {termName: number} map giving starting values for every DIFFERENTIAL term.

step6_visualization.const: Scan board for: ChartShape (xTerm + yTerms reveal what is plotted against what); ReferentialShape + child shapes (xTerm/yTerm of children reveal spatial motion); SliderShape.term (explorable parameters); ValueShape.term (key output displays).

step7_cases.const: If properties.casesCount > 1, the full equation system is solved independently for each case. Different initial values or parameter values per case enable parameter studies. ChartYTerm.case and TableColumn.case select which case a series displays.

step8_preloadedData.const: If preloadedData is present, external CSV data is loaded into calculator terms (names array maps to columns; values is rows × columns). This enables model–data comparison.

identifyingTheScience.description: How to infer the scientific principle from the expression patterns present in the model.

singleDecayODE.const: One DIFFERENTIAL term whose RHS contains only that term (dQ/dt = -k*Q) → exponential growth or decay. Domains: radioactive decay, bacterial growth, RC circuit charging, first-order chemical reaction, Newton's cooling.

secondOrderMechanics.const: dx/dt = v and dv/dt = f(x,v,t) → second-order dynamical system. dv/dt = -g → free fall. dv/dt = -omega^2 * x → SHM. dv/dt = -k/m*x - b/m*v → damped oscillation. dv/dt = F/m (piecewise F) → nonlinear mechanics.

projectile2D.const: Four ODEs: dx/dt=vx, dvx/dt=Fx/m, dy/dt=vy, dvy/dt=Fy/m → 2D planar mechanics. Visualized with ReferentialShape + BodyShape.

predatorPrey.const: Two ODEs where dX/dt = alpha*X - beta*X*Y and dY/dt = delta*X*Y - gamma*Y → Lotka-Volterra. Domains: predator-prey, competing species.

compartmental.const: Three or more ODEs where all terms flowing out of one compartment appear as positive inflows in another → SIR/SEIR epidemic model, pharmacokinetics, nutrient cycling.

logistic.const: dN/dt = r*N*(1 - N/K) → logistic growth with carrying capacity K. Domains: population ecology, resource-limited growth.

coupledOscillators.const: Multiple pairs of (dx/dt, dv/dt) ODEs linked through shared force terms → spring chains, pendulum networks, wave propagation.

noODEs.const: All terms are FUNCTION or PARAMETER with no DIFFERENTIAL terms → static/algebraic model. Domains: geometry exploration, algebraic identities, function graphing, discrete recurrence.

recurrence.const: Statements of the form name_{n} = f(name_{n-1}) → discrete-time recurrence. Domains: compound interest, discrete population models, iterative maps (logistic map).

buildingAModel.description: How to construct a new model from a science or math intent.

step1_equations.const: Identify the governing equations. Write as first-order ODEs (decompose second-order by introducing velocity). List physical constants as parameter assignments.

step3_expressions.const: Create ExpressionShape items: one for parameters (e.g. g=9.8, k=1, m=0.5), one for the dynamic equations, optionally one for derived quantities (energy, momentum, etc.). Place them at non-overlapping x/y positions on the canvas.

step4_initialValues.const: Set properties.initialValuesByCase["1"] with {termName: number} for every DIFFERENTIAL term. For multi-case models, add keys "2", "3", ... with different starting conditions.

step5_visualization.const: Add visualization shapes: ChartShape for time-series plots (xTerm: 't', yTerms listing key quantities); ReferentialShape + BodyShape for spatial motion (BodyShape.xTerm/yTerm bound to position terms); SliderShape for parameters users should explore; ValueShape for key output numbers.

step6_metadata.const: Set properties.name (brief title, e.g. 'Free Fall'), properties.description (1-2 sentences on the science), properties.instructions (Markdown guide for users).

step7_angleUnit.const: Set properties.angleUnit to 'radians' (default) or 'degrees'. This affects all trig functions and angle terms throughout the model.

stepSizeGuidance.description: How to choose properties.independent.step for numerical stability with Euler integration.

general.const: Euler method is first-order accurate. Smaller step = more accurate but slower. Typical: step = 0.01 to 0.1 for physical problems on a 0–10 time scale.

oscillation.const: For oscillatory systems: step < period / 100 for reasonable accuracy. SHM with omega=1 (period ≈ 6.28) → step ≤ 0.05.

fast_dynamics.const: If the RHS contains large coefficients (e.g. k=1000), the time scale is ~1/k = 0.001 — match the step to that scale.

rule_of_thumb.const: Safe default: step = (end - start) / 1000. Increase if the model is too slow; decrease if trajectories look jagged or diverge.

shapeRoles.description: Semantic role of each shape type in a model and how to configure it. Schema-only documentation.

ExpressionShape.const: Hosts LaTeX mathematical statements. properties.expression is a \displaylines{} block. All terms defined here feed the shared calculator. Use multiple shapes: one per conceptual group (parameters, equations, derived quantities). Canvas position matters only for readability.

TextShape.const: Static rich-text annotation. properties.text contains HTML. Use for titles, explanations, axis labels, or instructions. No calculator interaction.

ReferentialShape.const: Coordinate system frame for spatial visualization. Child shapes are positioned by calculator terms. Set autoScale:true to fit data. Set scaleX/scaleY for a fixed unit-per-pixel ratio. Essential for any model with x/y position terms.

BodyShape.const: Animated body (circle or character sprite) inside a ReferentialShape. parent = ReferentialShape id. xTerm and yTerm are calculator term names for position. trajectoryColor shows the motion trail. characterKey selects a sprite (see resources/characters/characters.json).

PointShape.const: Small dot inside a ReferentialShape tracking (xTerm, yTerm). Lighter than BodyShape when no sprite or trail is needed.

VectorShape.const: Animated arrow inside a ReferentialShape. xTerm/yTerm = vector components. xOriginTerm/yOriginTerm = base of arrow. Use to visualize force, velocity, or acceleration vectors.

LineShape.const: Infinite line inside a ReferentialShape defined by a point (xTerm, yTerm) and angleTerm.

ArcShape.const: Animated arc inside a ReferentialShape. xTerm/yTerm = center, radiusTerm = radius, startAngleTerm/endAngleTerm = sweep. Use for angles, sectors, pendulum bobs.

ChartShape.const: Line/scatter/area/bar chart. xTerm is usually the independent variable 't'. yTerms lists series: each entry has a term name and a case number. For multi-case comparisons add one entry per case with the same term name. autoScale:true fills the axis range automatically.

SliderShape.const: Interactive slider controlling a PARAMETER term. term = parameter name (e.g. 'g', 'k', 'm'). Set minimum/maximum to a sensible physical range. Users drag to explore parameter sensitivity.

ValueShape.const: Displays the current numeric value of any calculator term. term = term name. termDisplayMode: 'nameValue' shows label + number, 'valueOnly' shows only the number.

TableShape.const: Tabular view of term values across iterations. columns array lists term names. Useful for discrete/recurrence models or when exact numeric output matters.

GaugeShape.const: Polar gauge. angleTerm + magnitudeTerm drive the needle. Use for two-component quantities like velocity direction and speed.

MediaShape.const: Static or animated image, video or audio. Use for background reference images (e.g., a photograph to overlay a model trajectory) or synced/free-running video and audio.

RulerShape.const: On-canvas ruler for manual distance measurement. Not connected to calculator terms. Teaching aid.

ProtractorShape.const: On-canvas protractor for manual angle measurement. Not connected to calculator terms. Teaching aid.

modelTemplates.description: Complete model specifications for common STEM problems. Each template lists all fields an agent needs to construct a working model. Expression content references expression-schema latexTemplates for the LaTeX strings. Schema-only documentation.

principle.const: An object dropped from rest (or thrown vertically) under constant gravitational acceleration. Position x and velocity v change over time.

const.note: Plot height and velocity over time.

exploration.const: Change g (Moon: 1.6, Mars: 3.7, Jupiter: 24.8). Change initial x for different drop heights. Add a second case with a different initial v to compare.

principle.const: 2D projectile launched at an angle. Horizontal motion is uniform; vertical motion has constant gravitational deceleration.

const.note: Height and vertical velocity vs. time.

exploration.const: Vary initial vx and vy to see range and height. Use casesCount > 1 with different launch angles to compare trajectories. Add air resistance: change dvx/dt = -k*vx, dvy/dt = -g - k*vy.

principle.const: Spring-mass system. Restoring force proportional to displacement produces sinusoidal oscillation. Angular frequency omega = sqrt(k/m).

const.note: Phase portrait: ellipse in x-v plane.

exploration.const: Verify period T = 2*pi*sqrt(m/k). Check that total energy E is conserved. Add a damping term b: change dv/dt to -k/m*x - b/m*v and vary b from 0 to 2*sqrt(k*m) to show underdamped, critically damped, overdamped regimes.

principle.const: Spring-mass system with viscous damping b. Energy dissipates over time; the system transitions from underdamped to overdamped as b increases.

exploration.const: Drag b slider: b < 2 underdamped (oscillatory decay); b = 2 critically damped (fastest return without oscillation); b > 2 overdamped (slow exponential return). Use casesCount=3 with b=0.5, 2, 4 to compare regimes on one chart.

principle.const: Predator-prey population dynamics. Prey X grows exponentially in absence of predators; predators Y die in absence of prey. Their interaction produces coupled oscillations.

const.expression: \displaylines{\frac{dX}{dt}=\alpha\cdot X-\beta\cdot X\cdot Y\\\\\frac{dY}{dt}=\delta\cdot X\cdot Y-\gamma\cdot Y}

exploration.const: Observe that population cycles are out of phase (prey peaks before predators). Change alpha and gamma to see how growth/death rates affect cycle frequency. Start with X=Y=10 then try X=40, Y=2 to see larger orbits.

principle.const: SIR compartmental epidemic model. S = susceptible, I = infected, R = recovered. The basic reproduction number R0 = beta/gamma determines whether an epidemic occurs (R0 > 1).

const.expression: \displaylines{\frac{dS}{dt}=-\frac{\beta\cdot S\cdot I}{N}\\\\\frac{dI}{dt}=\frac{\beta\cdot S\cdot I}{N}-\gamma\cdot I\\\\\frac{dR}{dt}=\gamma\cdot I}

exploration.const: Drag beta and gamma and watch the R0 display. R0 < 1: no epidemic; R0 > 1: epidemic peak appears. Reduce beta to simulate social distancing. Increase gamma to simulate faster recovery (treatment). Note S + I + R = N is conserved.

principle.const: Newton's law of cooling: the rate of heat loss is proportional to the temperature difference between the object and the ambient environment.

exploration.const: Use casesCount=3 with initial T = 90, 60, 30 to show that all curves approach the same ambient. Verify the half-life formula t_half = ln(2)/k. Compare to preloaded experimental temperature data to fit k.

principle.const: Population grows exponentially when small but slows as it approaches the carrying capacity K. The inflection point occurs at N = K/2.

exploration.const: Change r to see how intrinsic growth rate affects the S-curve steepness. Change K to see the horizontal asymptote shift. Use casesCount=3 with N0 = 5, 50, 150 to show convergence to K from below and above.

principle.const: RC charging circuit. The capacitor voltage (or charge Q) approaches the supply voltage V exponentially. Time constant tau = R*C.

exploration.const: Verify tau = R*C by reading the time at which U reaches 63% of V. Use casesCount=3 with different R values to compare charging speeds. Add a discharge phase using a piecewise conditional for the supply: V active for t < 0.025, 0 after.

principle.const: Discrete-time compound interest. Principal P grows by factor (1+r) each iteration. The independent variable n represents compounding periods.

exploration.const: Use casesCount=3 with r=0.03, 0.07, 0.12 to compare growth rates. Note the doubling time ≈ 70/r% (rule of 70). Compare continuous growth (ODE: dP/dt = r*P) with discrete recurrence to see convergence as step -> 0.

ModelProperties.title: ModelProperties
ModelProperties.description: Top-level model configuration stored in shell.properties.

description.description: Optional description shown in the marketplace.

backgroundColor.description: Background color of the canvas (hex color string).

precision.description: Default number of decimal places shown in outputs.

iterationTerm.description: Name of the iteration counter term.

initialValuesByCase.description: Map of case index to initial term values. Keys are 1-based case numbers as strings.

additionalProperties.description: Map of term name to initial numeric value.

iterationDuration.description: Optional duration (in ms) per iteration step for animation playback.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL of the model thumbnail image.

instructions.description: Markdown instructions shown to users who open the model.

educationLevel.description: Target education level; affects UI simplification.

snapToGrid.description: Whether shapes snap to the grid when dragged.

IndependentVariable.title: IndependentVariable
IndependentVariable.description: Configuration of the independent variable (e.g. time).

name.description: Name of the independent variable term.

start.description: Starting value of the independent variable.

end.description: Ending value of the independent variable.

noLimit.description: When true, the simulation runs without an end value.

Board.title: Board
Board.description: Ordered array of all shapes on the canvas.

AnyShape.title: AnyShape
AnyShape.description: Discriminated union of all supported shape types.

BaseProperties.title: BaseProperties
BaseProperties.description: Properties shared by every shape (from BaseShape.setDefaults).

foregroundColor.description: Primary color used for strokes and text (hex).

showName.description: Whether the shape name label is rendered on canvas.

nameColor.description: Color of the name label. Inherits foregroundColor when null.

visibleToUsers.description: Whether the shape is visible to non-creator users.

lockedForUsers.description: Whether non-creator users can interact with the shape.

ChildProperties.title: ChildProperties
ChildProperties.description: Additional properties for shapes that live inside a ReferentialShape.

parentId.description: ID of the parent ReferentialShape. Null for top-level shapes.

trajectoryColor.description: Color of the motion trail drawn on the canvas (hex or 'transparent').

stroboscopyColor.description: Color of stroboscopy ghost copies (hex or 'transparent').

stroboscopyInterval.description: Number of iterations between stroboscopy frames.

TermDisplayMode.description: Controls whether a term value label is rendered next to the shape.

ExpressionShape.title: ExpressionShape
ExpressionShape.description: A mathematical expression entry box rendered as LaTeX.

expression.description: LaTeX content of the expression, using \displaylines{} for multi-line.

TextShape.title: TextShape
TextShape.description: A rich-text box rendered with the Quill editor.

text.description: HTML/Markdown content of the text box.

BodyShape.title: BodyShape
BodyShape.description: A physics body (circle or character sprite) that moves according to calculator terms.

xTerm.description: Calculator expression for the x coordinate in model units.

yTerm.description: Calculator expression for the y coordinate in model units.

sizeTerm.description: Calculator expression for the body radius in model units.

xTermLocked.description: When true, the x term cannot be changed by dragging.

yTermLocked.description: When true, the y term cannot be changed by dragging.

radius.description: Computed radius in canvas pixels (derived from sizeTerm at runtime).

angle.description: Visual rotation of the body sprite in degrees.

animationFrameStep.description: Number of simulation steps per animation frame for character sprites.

imageUrl.description: URL of a custom image asset.

imageBase64.description: Base-64 encoded image data (used when imageUrl is empty).

characterKey.description: Key matching a folder name in resources/characters/characters.json.

dropShadow.description: Whether to render a drop shadow beneath the body.

showCenter.description: Whether to show a center-point marker on the body.

nameIsDefault.description: True when the name has not been changed by the user.

isPhysical.description: When true, the body participates in physics calculations.

ChartShape.title: ChartShape
ChartShape.description: A line/scatter/area/bar chart plotting one or more calculator terms.

xTerm.description: Term name plotted on the x axis.

autoScale.description: When true, axes are auto-scaled to fit data.

equalScales.description: When true, x and y scales are constrained to be equal.

tangentColor.description: Color of the tangent line overlay (hex with alpha).

yTerms.description: One entry per series plotted on the y axis.

term.description: Calculator term name for this series.

color.description: Series color (hex or empty string for theme default).

showLabel.description: Whether to show the series name as a data label.

SliderShape.title: SliderShape
SliderShape.description: An interactive vertical slider that controls a calculator term.

term.description: Calculator term name controlled by this slider.

autoScale.description: When true, min/max auto-adjust to encompass data range.

fillColor.description: Fill color of the slider track (hex).

precision.description: Number of decimal places for the slider value.

ValueShape.title: ValueShape
ValueShape.description: A display box showing the current value of a calculator term.

termCase.description: 1-based case index for the displayed term.

fontSizeTerm.description: Font size in pixels, or a calculator expression that evaluates to a size.

termDisplayMode.description: Controls which parts of the term (name, value, or both) are rendered.

soundInstrument.description: Web Audio oscillator type used to sonify the term value.

TableShape.title: TableShape
TableShape.description: A data table displaying columns of calculator term values over time.

columnWidths.description: Per-column pixel widths. Empty array means equal widths.

headerBackgroundColor.description: Background color of the header row (hex).

columns.description: Ordered list of columns to display.

term.description: Calculator term name for this column.

color.description: Accent color for this column (hex or 'transparent').

valueDisplayMode.description: Mini-chart style rendered inside each cell.

MediaShape.title: MediaShape
MediaShape.description: A static image, video or audio element placed on the canvas.

videoStepsPerFrame.description: Number of simulation steps per video/audio frame.

lockAspectRatio.description: When true, resizing preserves the media aspect ratio.

mediaAspectRatio.description: Width-to-height ratio of the loaded media (0 when not yet loaded).

mediaSynced.description: When true, video/audio playback is synced with the simulation player. When false, hovering shows native media controls.

PointShape.title: PointShape
PointShape.description: A small dot that tracks the position of a calculator term within a referential.

xTerm.description: Calculator expression for the x coordinate in model units.

yTerm.description: Calculator expression for the y coordinate in model units.

radius.description: Radius of the dot in canvas pixels.

LineShape.title: LineShape
LineShape.description: An infinite line passing through a point at a given angle within a referential.

xTerm.description: Calculator expression for the x coordinate of a point on the line.

yTerm.description: Calculator expression for the y coordinate of a point on the line.

angleTerm.description: Calculator expression for the line angle (in model angle units).

angle.description: Computed angle in model angle units (derived from angleTerm at runtime).

VectorShape.title: VectorShape
VectorShape.description: An arrow from an origin point to a tip defined by x/y component terms.

xTerm.description: Calculator expression for the x component (model units).

yTerm.description: Calculator expression for the y component (model units).

xOriginTerm.description: Calculator expression for the x coordinate of the vector origin.

yOriginTerm.description: Calculator expression for the y coordinate of the vector origin.

startTipType.description: Arrowhead style at the origin end.

endTipType.description: Arrowhead style at the tip end.

showComponents.description: When true, horizontal and vertical component projections are shown.

ArcShape.title: ArcShape
ArcShape.description: A circular arc defined by a centre, radius and two angle terms.

xTerm.description: Calculator expression for the centre x coordinate.

yTerm.description: Calculator expression for the centre y coordinate.

radiusTerm.description: Calculator expression for the arc radius in model units.

startAngleTerm.description: Calculator expression for the start angle in model angle units.

startAngle.description: Computed start angle in model angle units.

endAngleTerm.description: Calculator expression for the end angle in model angle units.

endAngle.description: Computed end angle in model angle units.

GaugeShape.title: GaugeShape
GaugeShape.description: A polar gauge with an angle axis and a magnitude axis.

angleTerm.description: Calculator term controlling the needle angle.

magnitudeTerm.description: Calculator term controlling the needle magnitude.

startAngle.description: Start of the gauge sweep in degrees.

endAngle.description: End of the gauge sweep in degrees.

anglePrecision.description: Tick interval in degrees for the angle axis.

magnitudePrecision.description: Tick count for the magnitude axis.

RulerShape.title: RulerShape
RulerShape.description: A physical ruler overlay used to measure distances on the canvas.

minimum.description: Start value shown at the ruler origin.

maximum.description: End value shown at the ruler end.

majorTicks.description: Number of major intervals used across the ruler.

ProtractorShape.title: ProtractorShape
ProtractorShape.description: A physical protractor overlay used to measure angles on the canvas.

startAngle.description: Start of the visible arc in model angle units.

endAngle.description: End of the visible arc in model angle units (π or 180 depending on angleUnit).

ReferentialShape.title: ReferentialShape
ReferentialShape.description: A coordinate system frame that hosts child shapes (points, vectors, arcs, lines, bodies, images).

originX.description: x offset of the mathematical origin within the shape bounding box (canvas pixels).

originY.description: y offset of the mathematical origin within the shape bounding box (canvas pixels).

scaleX.description: Model units per canvas pixel along the x axis.

scaleY.description: Model units per canvas pixel along the y axis.

autoScale.description: When true, scaleX/scaleY auto-fit to the data on each calculation.

equalAxisScales.description: When true, scaleX and scaleY are kept equal.

showTicksWithValues.description: Whether axis tick labels are rendered.

snapToTicks.description: When true, child shapes snap to the referential tick grid when dragged.

backgroundImageUrl.description: URL of an optional background image shown inside the referential.

PreloadedData.title: PreloadedData
PreloadedData.description: External data imported from a CSV file or URL, loaded into calculator terms.

names.description: Ordered list of term names corresponding to CSV columns.

values.description: 2-D array of rows × columns of numeric values.

OutlierIterations.title: OutlierIterations
OutlierIterations.description: Set of iteration indices excluded from preloaded data for each term.

termName.description: Name of the term whose outlier rows are stored.

iterations.description: 0-based row indices of outlier data points.

RegressionTerms.title: RegressionTerms
RegressionTerms.description: List of regression term definitions computed from preloaded data.

targetTermName.description: Name of the term whose values are predicted by the regression.

sourceTermName.description: Name of the independent-variable term used as the regression input.

ranges.description: One entry per case/range combination fitted.

expressionLatex.description: LaTeX representation of the fitted regression expression.

caseNumber.description: 1-based case index this range applies to.

regressionType.description: Polynomial degree of the regression fit.

independentStart.description: Lower bound of the independent variable range used for fitting.

independentEnd.description: Upper bound of the independent variable range used for fitting.
